content.full_text:
August 25, 1992


Pauline Roccucci, Councilmember
911 Herbert Street
Roseville, CA 95678

                                        Re:   Your Request for Advice
                                              Our File No. A-92-428

Dear Councilmember Roccucci:

     You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

     In your letter of July 24, 1992, you have asked what
procedure to follow when a credit in the amount of $204.46 has
been received after the campaign bank account established for your
election has been closed.

     The Act requires all contributions or loans made to the
candidate, or to the candidate's controlled committee be deposited
into a campaign bank account.  (Section 85201(c).)  In addition,
all expenditures must be made from the campaign bank account.
(Section 85201(e).)  Therefore, you will need to establish a
campaign bank account at a financial institution located within
California.  Within 10 days of opening the account, you must file
a Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502) with the Commission.
(Section 85201(b).)

     Miscellaneous receipts of $25 or more must be accounted for
on your recordkeeping system, with the date of the receipt,
amount, name and address of the source, and description of the
miscellaneous receipt.  (Campaign Disclosure Information Manual A,
page 62.)


_________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95812  •  (916) 322-5660

Pauline Roccucci
"A-92-428
Page 2


     I have enclosed a Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502)
and a Campaign Disclosure Information Manual A for your
convenience.  If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5662.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Assistant Chief Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Sandra L. Taylor
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

sections.question: What procedure to follow when a credit in the amount of $204.46 has been received after the campaign bank account established for your election has been closed.
sections.conclusion: You will need to establish a campaign bank account at a financial institution located within California. Within 10 days of opening the account, you must file a Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502) with the Commission. Miscellaneous receipts of $25 or more must be accounted for on your recordkeeping system, with the date of the receipt, amount, name and address of the source, and description of the miscellaneous receipt.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks what procedures must be followed when a campaign receives a credit/miscellaneous receipt of $204.46 after the campaign bank account has already been closed.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The candidate must reestablish a campaign bank account within California and file Form 502 within 10 days. The $204.46 credit must be recorded in the recordkeeping system with required details including date, amount, source information, and description.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard FPPC advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Requestor identified as Pauline Roccucci, Councilmember from Roseville. Document references Political Reform Act sections and includes enclosures of relevant forms.

QUESTION: What procedure to follow when a credit in the amount of $204.46 has been received after the campaign bank account established for your election has been closed.

CONCLUSION: You will need to establish a campaign bank account at a financial institution located within California. Within 10 days of opening the account, you must file a Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502) with the Commission. Miscellaneous receipts of $25 or more must be accounted for on your recordkeeping system, with the date of the receipt, amount, name and address of the source, and description of the miscellaneous receipt.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice to Councilmember Roccucci regarding proper handling of a late campaign credit received after the campaign bank account closure, requiring establishment of a new campaign bank account and filing of Form 502 with appropriate recordkeeping.
